message:
fix(engine): simplify allocator APIs and prevent integer overflow in entropy estimator

1. baseline_interpreter.rs — revert alloc_object/alloc_iterator to infallible:
   The previous commit made alloc_object_with_prototype fallible (returning
   Result), but callers like alloc_object() and alloc_iterator() were in
   contexts where propagating errors added complexity without benefit (tests,
   initialization). Reverted alloc_object() and alloc_iterator() back to
   panicking wrappers with .expect(), while keeping the inner
   alloc_object_with_prototype() fallible for the interpreter loop. Updated
   test call sites to use .unwrap() on alloc_object_with_prototype().

2. entropy_evidence_compressor.rs — prevent i64 overflow in frequency math:
   EntropyEstimator::frequency_millionths() and ArithmeticCoder's Kraft sum
   computed `count * MILLION` in i64, which overflows when count exceeds
   ~9.2 × 10¹². Widened to i128 intermediate arithmetic. Also guards
   against division by zero with .max(1) on denominators.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
